title: Bellingdon Lancaster Memorial
memorial_type: Blue circular plaque with central illustration of Lancaster in flight, set into large stone

external_narratives.summary: IWM records the Lancaster PB475 memorial at Bellingdon, Buckinghamshire, noting the stone and plaque memorial dedicated to the crew who died at Bloomfield Farm.

ASN records Lancaster PB475 crashed on 6 March 1945 at 01:00 hours near Bellingdon, Buckinghamshire. The aircraft departed RAF Little Staughton at 17:33 for a bombing raid on Chemnitz. A hung-up target indicator ignited in the bomb bay during the return flight.
key_details: Hung-up target indicator ignited in bomb bay
external_narratives.notes: URL dead as of 2026-04-06; URL dead as of 2026-04-06

external_narratives.summary: Article documenting the PB475 crash at Bloomfield Farm, noting the aircraft was just 10 minutes from landing when the target indicator ignited. The stone and plaque memorial was dedicated on 13 May 2012.
key_details: Crew included Barnett, Ross Denbigh, Gould, Hemsworth, Ralph, and Torr
external_narratives.notes: URL dead as of 2026-04-06; URL dead as of 2026-04-06

local_contacts.role: Local authority responsible for memorial upkeep

sources: IWM War Memorials Register ref 63847 — OS Grid SP 93606 05127
location_confidence.verification_notes: SEVERE ERROR. Current coords (51.6492, -0.7822) are 12km SW of actual location — likely data entry error. Crash site memorial at Bloomfield Farm, Bellingdon HP5 2UT. Stone with blue circular plaque featuring Lancaster illustration. Dedicated 13 May 2012 by survivors' families. Aircraft was 582 Squadron, Little Staughton (current record says 'Bomber Command' only — squadron should be updated). Crashed 0100hrs 6 March 1945 returning from Chemnitz. 6 of 7 crew killed.
